title: 3 Legal Considerations
body_text:
3	Legal Considerations
3.1	Overview of Legal Considerations
Financial institutions are responsible for complying with applicable legislation and regulations. The information provided in this Chapter is for convenience only and does not take the place of statutes and regulations. It is recommended that reference is made to the actual statute or regulation for the full text.
Note:	Other federal and provincial legislation influences and regulates AFT Transactions (e.g., privacy legislation; human rights legislation). The financial institution must apply additional policies and procedures to address this.
Note:	This Chapter describes legal considerations specific to AFT Transactions. Subscribers to the Operations Manual can refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part, for detailed AML information on:
â Legal considerations (includes topics in addition to AFT Transactions)
â Appointing an AML compliance officer and risk assessment team
â Establishing
PCMLTFA
compliance policies
â Establishing FINTRAC reporting policies
3.2	CPA Rules
The Canadian Payment Association (CPA) develops and implements the rules that apply to the clearing and settlement of different types of payments between its member financial institutions. See the CPA website for more information on CPA Rules.
CPA Rule F1, Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions
â Includes general rules relating to the exchange of AFT Files, procedures applicable to the processing of AFT transactions, as well as the settlement of obligations arising from the exchange and clearing of AFT debits and credits between Direct Clearers. It also contains procedures applicable to exception processing, problem resolution, contingency situations, and escalation procedures in the AFT environment.
CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits
â Outlines the procedures for the exchange for the purpose of clearing and settlement of every Pre-authorized Debit (PAD) (i.e., Business PAD, Cash Management PAD, Funds Transfer PAD, and personal PAD).
CPA Standards 005, Standards for the Exchange of Financial Data on AFT Files
â Addresses the exchange of financial data by data transmission. This standard is to be read in conjunction with CPA Rule F1.
CPA Standard 007, Standards for the Use of Transaction Codes and Return Reason Codes in AFT Files
â Lists valid AFT transaction codes and AFT return reason codes.
3.3	Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act and Regulations (Canada)
PCMLTFA
and
PCMLTF Regulations
apply to financial institutions and their agents (e.g., deposit taking agents). Contact your financial institutionâs AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for more information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
3.4	Restricted Entities
Financial institutions must exercise enhanced due diligence to identify Restricted Entities when processing applications from new Business Originators. While due diligence is ultimately the financial institutionâs responsibility, Central 1 reserves the right to further investigate Restricted Entities and may decline to provide services to entities deemed to be outside of Central 1âs risk appetite. Central 1 may make inquiries on specific transactions and ask for the results of the financial institutionâs enhanced due diligence efforts.
END
